channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Nostalgia, relief and loss as some Syrians mark their first Ramadan back home in years
item.description: DARAYA, Syria (AP) — When Mariam Aabour learned of the ouster of Syrian leader Bashar Assad, she shed tears of joy. But as the time came to return to her homeland from Lebanon – where she fled years earlier – Aabour felt torn.
item.dc:creator: MARIAM FAM, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Danish foreign minister scolds Trump administration for its criticism of Denmark and Greenland
item.description: NUUK, Greenland (AP) — The Danish foreign minister on Saturday scolded the Trump administration for its “tone” in criticizing Denmark and Greenland, saying his country is already investing more into Arctic security and remains open to more cooperation with the U.S.
item.dc:creator: PHILIP CROWTHER, KIRSTEN GRIESHABER and AAMER MADHANI, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: United Kingdom could be only G7 nation not to produce its own steel; Chinese owner blames Trump tariffs
item.description: A Chinese steel group that owns British Steel is blaming U.S. tariffs for the potential closure of two blast furnaces that could impact the steel industry in Britain.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Film academy apologizes for not naming 'No Other Land' co-director in response to attack on him
item.description: The organization that bestows the Academy Awards has apologized to “No Other Land” co-director Hamdan Ballal after mounting criticism following its response to the violent attack on the Oscar winner.
item.dc:creator: Jake Coyle, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Israeli settlers seen on camera assaulting a Palestinian village. Police arrest only Palestinians
item.description: JERUSALEM (AP) — Over a dozen Israeli settlers attacked a Palestinian village in the southern Israeli-occupied West Bank on Friday, beating residents with sticks and rocks, in an incident captured with rare clarity by security cameras.
item.dc:creator: JULIA FRANKEL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Vance slams Denmark's handling of Greenland security
item.description: Vice President Vance on Friday slammed Denmark as failing to prioritize Greenland’s security, escalating rhetoric between Washington and Copenhagen amid President Trump’s claims to take over the island.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: Canadian company seeks US permission to start deep-sea mining as outcry ensues
item.description: An abrupt announcement has rattled members of a little-known U.N. agency based in Jamaica that has protected deep international waters for more than 30 years.
item.dc:creator: Dánica Coto, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Trump administration moves to eliminate USAID, firing remaining employees
item.description: The Trump administration is moving to formally end the U.S. Agency for International Development (USAID), notifying the remaining employees they will be terminated and the agency will be merged with the State Department.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, The Hill

item.title: Myanmar at a glance: Embroiled in civil war, now facing more devastation after powerful earthquake
item.description: Even before a powerful 7.7-magnitude quake hit Myanmar on Friday, more than 3 million people in the country had been displaced, and hundreds of thousands were cut off from vital food and health programs as a result of a destructive four-year civil war that international groups claim has indiscrimina
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: What to know about the US military's Pituffik Space Base in Greenland
item.description: NUUK, Greenland (AP) — The remote Pituffik Space Base in northwestern Greenland, which Vice President JD Vance is visiting on Friday, is the U.S. Department of Defense's northernmost installation. The base was built following a 1951 defense agreement between Denmark and the United States.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Putin says Trump plans for Greenland 'has nothing to do' with Russia
item.description: Russian President Vladimir Putin says President Donald Trump's ambitions for Greenland has "nothing to do" with Russia as Vice President JD Vance lands to assess security base.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: European institutions target 'scientific asylum-seekers' from US
item.description: The move comes as federal funds for research are cut and studies are subject to ideological limitations.
item.dc:creator: Steph Whiteside, The Hill

item.title: South Korea's worst wildfires are now almost contained following rain and cooler weather
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — The most destructive wildfires ever to hit South Korea were almost contained, authorities announced Friday, after rain and cooler temperatures helped fire crew put out the blazes that have killed 28 people and razed vast swaths of land since last week.
item.dc:creator: HYUNG-JIN KIM and KIM TONG-HYUNG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Suspected US strikes pummel Houthi-controlled areas of Yemen
item.description: DUBAI, United Arab Emirates (AP) — Suspected U.S. airstrikes pummeled sites across Yemen controlled by the Houthi rebels early Friday, including neighborhoods in the capital, Sanaa.
item.dc:creator: JON GAMBRELL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Vance accuses Denmark of underinvesting in Greenland as Trump presses for US takeover of the island
item.description: U.S. Vice President JD Vance says Denmark has “underinvested” in Greenland’s security and demands Denmark change its approach as President Donald Trump continues to talk of taking over the Danish territory.
item.dc:creator: Kirsten Grieshaber, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: King Charles III briefly hospitalized for observation following side effects from cancer treatment
item.description: LONDON (AP) — King Charles III was briefly hospitalized for observation on Thursday after experiencing “temporary side effects’’ related to a scheduled cancer treatment, Buckingham Palace said in a statement. His engagements for Thursday afternoon and Friday were canceled.
item.dc:creator: DANICA KIRKA, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: World Central Kitchen says Israeli strike killed Gaza volunteer
item.description: The World Central Kitchen (WCK) said that an Israeli military strike killed one of the charity group’s volunteers working in the Gaza Strip and injured six other people. “Our hearts are heavy today as we mourn the loss of one of our volunteers in Gaza.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: Ex-President Martinelli gets Panama's permission to take asylum in Nicaragua
item.description: PANAMA CITY (AP) — Ex-Panama President Ricardo Martinelli will be allowed to leave the Nicaraguan embassy where he’s been holed up for more than a year to travel to Nicaragua for humanitarian reasons, Panama Foreign Affairs Minister Javier Martínez-Acha Vásquez said Thursday.
item.dc:creator: ALMA SOLÍS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: New 2-clawed dinosaur discovered in Mongolia
item.description: Although the new species has large front claws, it is likely a herbivore who used them to grab leaves.
item.dc:creator: Steph Whiteside, NewsNation

item.title: Europe says no to Trump push to lift sanctions on Russia, upending Black Sea ceasefire
item.description: Europe says no to President Donald Trump's attempts to secure some sanction relief for Russia as he tries to make a Black Sea ceasefire deal between Russia and Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: 6 dead after tourist submarine sinks off Egypt's coast: officials
item.description: Six dead, dozens rescued after tourist submarine sinks off the coast on Egypt on Thursday. Officials say all of the victims were Russian. It is not known what caused the sinking.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Palestinians protest Hamas in a rare public show of dissent in Gaza
item.description: CAIRO (AP) — Thousands of Palestinians marched between the wreckage of a heavily destroyed town in northern Gaza on Wednesday in the second day of anti-war protests, with many chanting against Hamas in a rare display of public anger against the militant group.
item.dc:creator: SAMY MAGDY, FATMA KHALED and SARAH EL DEEB, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: The Atlantic releases full Signal text chain
item.description: The Atlantic editor-in-chief on Wednesday released the full text message chain between Trump administration officials.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, NewsNation

item.title: Gabbard, Ratcliffe, Patel criticized over Signal chat at hearing
item.description: Though the hearing is about the 2025 Annual Worldwide Threats Assessment, officials were questioned about a Signal group chat leak.
item.dc:creator: Kellie Meyer, NewsNation

item.title: JD Vance joining his wife for Greenland trip on Friday
item.description: The delegation is set to tour the northernmost security installation for the U.S. military, the Space Force's Pituffik Space Base.
item.dc:creator: Tom Dempsey, NewsNation

item.title: US holds separate talks with Russians after meeting Ukrainians to discuss a potential ceasefire
item.description: DUBAI, United Arab Emirates (AP) — U.S. negotiators worked on a proposed partial ceasefire in the 3-year-old war in Ukraine on Monday, meeting representatives from Russia one day after holding separate talks with a team from Kyiv.
item.dc:creator: JON GAMBRELL and ILLIA NOVIKOV, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: UN to reduce staff in Gaza, blaming Israel for a strike that killed its employee
item.description: DEIR AL-BALAH, Gaza Strip (AP) — The United Nations said Monday it will “reduce its footprint” in the Gaza Strip after an Israeli tank strike hit one of its compounds last week, killing one staffer from Bulgaria and wounding five other employees.
item.dc:creator: WAFAA SHURAFA, EDITH M. LEDERER and LEE KEATH, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: US, Russian officials meet in Saudi Arabia to talk ceasefire
item.description: Officials hope the talks will allow negotiators to iron out details of a possible limited ceasefire between Russia and Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: Robert Sherman, NewsNation

item.title: Gaza's Health Ministry says Israel has struck the largest hospital in the territory's south
item.description: DEIR AL-BALAH, Gaza Strip (AP) — Israel’s military struck the largest hospital in southern Gaza on Sunday night, killing two people, wounding others and causing a large fire, the territory's Health Ministry said.
item.dc:creator: WAFAA SHURAFA and SAMY MAGDY, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Oleg Gordievsky, Britain's most valuable Cold War spy inside the KGB, dies at 86
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Oleg Gordievsky, a Soviet KGB officer who helped change the course of the Cold War by covertly passing secrets to Britain, has died. He was 86. Gordievsky died March 4 in England, where he had lived since defecting in 1985.
item.dc:creator: JILL LAWLESS, Associated Press, NewsNation
